Oracle Events
- Search emails (via search_emails) for "Elena" to find Elena's email directives, including the AWS cost spike response and any executive-level communications about directives.
- Search Slack messages (via conversations_search_messages) for "ExpenseBot" to find Elena's pilot pause directive, the audit findings (47 expenses, 17% error rate), Dmitri's audit results identifying 5 false Vectral denials and 3 false Mosaic approvals, and the remediation status.
- Search Slack messages for "DOT compliance" to find Elena's hazmat documentation mandate, the 4-category checklist requirement, Chloe's implementation assignments, and Marcus's invoicing gate responsibility.
- Search Slack messages for "stipend fraud" to find Elena's $100 manual verification policy directive, Marcus's ownership of the policy document (due EOD 3/17), and the TechComfort Supply investigation.
- Search Slack messages for "Vectral" to find Elena's 10% Preferred Partner counter-proposal authorization, Mina's negotiation with Rachel Whitfield (who counter-proposed 12%), the final terms sent, and the CRM/QuickBooks estimate ($58,400) confirmation.
- Search Slack messages for "Terraform" to find Elena's April account assignment decisions - Mina as AM, coordinator hire authorization, Catalina's apology directive, tiered pricing instructions, and role requirements draft assignment.
- Search Linear issues (via linear_list_issues) to retrieve all open tickets and identify ExpenseBot remediation tickets (ENG-215, ENG-216, ENG-217, ENG-218, ENG-220), DOT compliance tickets, and their statuses, assignees, priorities, and due dates.
- Search emails for "ExpenseBot" to find Marcus's email demanding the expansion freeze and the audit completion requirements.
- Search emails for "DOT compliance" to find any follow-up communications about the hazmat documentation mandate and PHMSA extension.
- Search Slack messages for "AWS cost alert infrastructure estimator" to find the status of Elena's AWS cost controls mandate - Lena's CloudWatch setup progress, and whether cost alerting is fully operational.
- Search Slack messages for "Mosaic damage claim prototype" to find Emeka's incident report about the $90K damaged robotic arm prototype, the $50K insurance cap, and the resulting $40K liability gap that hasn't been escalated to Elena.
- Search emails for "pricing model review" to find follow-up on Elena's mandate for a pricing model review meeting and the >500 employee engineering cost approval rule.
- Search Slack messages for "training session hazmat coordinators" and "Chloe checklist template Airtable DOT" to verify whether Chloe's DOT implementation deliverables (checklist template, Airtable verification step, coordinator training) were actually completed.
- Search Slack messages for "invoicing gate compliance sign-off Marcus" to verify whether Marcus implemented the DOT invoicing compliance gate.
- Search Slack messages for "Catalina apology Rachel Terraform" and search emails for "apology Rachel Terraform" to check whether Catalina sent the personal apology Elena ordered "within the hour."
- Search Slack messages for "Marcus pricing sheet one-page Alejandro formalize" to check whether the tiered pricing one-pager for Terraform was completed.
- Search Slack messages for "Maria Santos Lisa Park James Okafor manual approve" to check whether the 3 unresolved Vectral false denials (Santos $550 standing desk, Okafor $82 keyboard tray, Park $130 mesh WiFi) have been corrected by Mina.
- Search Slack messages for "role requirements draft coordinator" to check whether Mina and Chloe completed the dedicated Terraform coordinator role requirements by end of week.
- Search Slack messages for "DOT extension June 5 submission" to find Marcus's update on the PHMSA extension and the $50K contingent liability accrual (ACCRUAL-DOT-2026-001).
- Send an executive briefing email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com containing the full 6-directive status tracker, dropped items, and un-escalated risks (Mosaic $40K gap, EMEA reconciliation risk, Mina bandwidth) for Elena's Monday leadership sync.
- Send a heads-up email (via send_email) from priya.chakrabarti@moveops.com to david.chen@moveops.com flagging 5 specific action items (Catalina's apology, coordinator role requirements, pricing sheet, 3 unresolved Vectral denials, interim coordinator assignment) plus a recommendation to brief Elena 1:1 on the Mosaic $40K damage claim before the group meeting.

7 The final response notes that the written stipend reimbursement policy document - which Elena assigned to Marcus Thorne with a deadline of EOD Monday, March 17 - has no evidence of having been completed, documented, or distributed to the account managers (Mina, Emeka, Catalina)
outcome
 ✗——✓✓✓ 60%
✗Run #1The agent marks the Stipend Fraud Policy as '✅ COMPLETE (per Marcus's Slack confirmation — policy doc prepared, Samira looped in...)' without identifying that there is no evidence the revised policy was distributed to AMs (Mina, Emeka, Catalina). The criterion requires flagging that this deliverable has no evidence of completion/distribution.
—Run #2No justification
—Run #3The agent notes the stipend policy is 'LIKELY DONE but unconfirmed' and that there is no follow-up confirmation the written policy was distributed to AMs. However, the agent treats this as 'likely done' rather than flagging it as a clear dropped deliverable with no evidence of completion. It partially meets the criterion but understates the risk.
✓Run #4The agent clearly identified that Marcus's written stipend reimbursement policy was never distributed, that the Airtable checkpoint was unestablished, and that AMs were never formally briefed — noting it was overdue since the EOD March 17 deadline.
✓Run #5The executive briefing email explicitly flags that 'No evidence found that the revised policy document was actually distributed to the AM team' and the David heads-up email also calls out that 'Marcus committed to delivering a revised stipend reimbursement policy document by EOD Monday 3/17 and briefing the AM team. I can't confirm it was circulated.'

9 The final response identifies the Mosaic Robotics approximately $40,000 insurance coverage gap as an un-escalated financial risk - specifically that MoveOps' vendor liability insurance caps at approximately $50,000 per item while the damaged robotic arm prototype (from Zara Okoye's Savannah-to-Atlanta relocation) is valued at approximately $90,000, creating a potential approximately $40,000 direct exposure for MoveOps
outcome
 ✗—✗✗✗✓ 20%
✗Run #1The agent never identifies the ~$40K insurance coverage gap (MoveOps vendor liability caps at ~$50K while Mosaic's damaged robotic arm prototype is valued at ~$90K). The Mosaic risk flagged is only the $885 false approval disclosure, not the $40K financial exposure.
—Run #2No justification
✗Run #3Neither the final response nor any of the emails mention the Mosaic Robotics ~$40,000 insurance coverage gap (vendor liability capped at $50K vs. $90K prototype value). This critical un-escalated financial risk was not identified at all.
✗Run #4The agent never mentions the ~$40,000 insurance coverage gap (MoveOps' $50K cap vs. the $90K Mosaic robotic arm prototype value). It only references the $1,550 ExpenseBot false approvals for Mosaic, a separate issue. The $40K liability exposure is absent from both the briefing email and final response.
✗Run #5Neither the executive briefing email, the David heads-up email, nor the final response mentions the Mosaic Robotics $40K insurance coverage gap (the $50K cap vs $90K prototype value). This critical un-escalated financial risk is entirely absent from the agent's output.
✓Run #6The briefing email identifies the Mosaic Robotics damage claim as an unescalated risk: '$90K prototype arrived damaged, vendor insurance covers only $50K, leaving a $40K gap.' The David heads-up also explicitly mentions this $40K liability gap.
10 The final response identifies that the Vectral Systems contract renewal is at risk or pending, with Rachel Whitfield's decision expected around her board meeting (\~April 23).
outcome
 ✓—✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response clearly identifies Vectral renewal as at risk, noting Rachel's board meeting was April 23 and no response was found, calling it 'the #1 thing Elena will ask about.' Rachel's decision status is flagged as unknown.
—Run #2No justification
✓Run #3The briefing email identifies the Vectral renewal risk, noting Rachel's 12% counter-offer needs an answer by April 22 for her board meeting on April 23, and that failing to respond could mean 'losing Vectral.' This meets the criterion.
✓Run #4The agent flags this as critical — noting Mina's final 10% offer went out, Rachel's board meeting was April 23, and there's zero response or follow-up. The Vectral renewal is labeled 'CRITICAL' in the briefing.
✓Run #5The briefing correctly identifies that Rachel's board meeting is April 23 with a decision deadline of April 22, and flags it as 'WATCH: Awaiting Rachel's final response. If she hasn't replied by Monday, Elena should know.' The Vectral renewal risk is clearly identified.
✓Run #6The tracker clearly shows the Vectral Counter-Proposal as '✅ Done — Awaiting Rachel's final decision' and specifies 'Rachel's board meeting ~April 23. Final answer expected by end of that week.' CRM status 'Renewal Pending' is mentioned.
11 The final response identifies that key Terraform Digital account action items - such as Catalina Dubois' personal apology email to Rachel Nguyen, the dedicated coordinator role requirements draft (assigned to Mina and Chloe), the interim coordinator assignment, or the one-page pricing sheet (assigned to Marcus and Alejandro) - appear stalled, dropped, or have no confirmation of completion.
